body.title: Fix tests that hang indefinitely (now surfaced by pytest-timeout)
body.result: Resolved in-code. Full-suite run today (3844 passed, 30 skipped, 15 warnings in 286.81s under IGOR_HOME_DB_URL + pytest.ini timeout=30): grep for '+++ Timeout +++' returns 0. The hangs surfaced at T-pytest-runner-pipe-hang land have been fixed by subsequent test/source work (no single commit attributable — natural cleanup through the pass-2 batch). Test plan success criterion met: N→0.
body.description:
After T-pytest-runner-pipe-hang shipped pytest.ini with timeout=30, at least 3-4 tests now show `+++ Timeout +++` markers instead of hanging the runner forever. Full stack traces point at `self._cond.wait(timeout)` and `self._selector.poll(timeout)` — looks like threading/socket waits in test code.

**Scope:** identify each hanging test (grep `+++ Timeout +++` in a full pytest run), root-cause each (mock/fixture missing a close? real network call in a unit test? deadlock?), and either fix the test or mark it skip/xfail with a linked ticket for the underlying code fix.

**Affected files:** tests/* (specific files TBD by running pytest and collecting timeout markers); possibly source files if fixes belong there

**Design rules:** docs-in-code (doesn't apply — test fixes, not load-bearing docs); test-plan-or-why-not (applies — plan: before/after count of `+++ Timeout +++` markers should go to 0); others don't apply directly

**Scope boundary:** In: tests currently showing timeout markers. Out: test coverage improvements, test refactors not related to hangs, fixing tests that merely fail (non-hang failures like test_pe_chain's TestPeStepRegistration are separate tickets).

**Test plan:** Before: `python -m pytest tests/ -q 2>&1 | grep -c '+++ Timeout +++'` returns N. After fix: returns 0. Also: `time python -m pytest tests/ -q` drops from 1m38s (with -x bailing) to whatever the full-suite time is, proportional to how many were hanging.
